non_claims.text: This workflow is a semantic gap and tree-evidence report; it does not prove the whole document.

LeanDojo, Pantograph, retrieval, route plans, and static extraction are diagnostic until direct Lean or another certifying backend checks the scoped target.

how_derivation_can_work.detail: Specify the kernel or conditional distribution used by the expectation.
Verify each random payoff, value, or derivative term has a finite conditional expectation.
Only after those checks should the equality be treated as a scalar derivation step.
Write the action path and baseline path under the same information set.
Show the incremental cash-flow term equals the exhaustive component sum with a single sign convention.
Apply the stated discount factors and terminal-value convention over the finite horizon.

semantic_work_packet.label: eq:panel-npv-functional

missing_obligations.closes: Makes the expectation operator well defined.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: A conditional probability law for the random variables inside the expectation.
missing_obligations.why_missing: A conditional expectation is not a real-valued operator until the conditioning law or kernel is specified.

missing_obligations.closes: Turns the displayed expression into a finite scalar equality.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: Measurability and finite conditional first moments for every payoff, value, or derivative term inside the expectation.
missing_obligations.why_missing: Without measurability and integrability, the expectation may be undefined or infinite.

missing_obligations.closes: Fixes the scope of the conditional expectation used in the derivation.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: A definition of the conditioning information set, state, or sigma-field.
missing_obligations.why_missing: The notation after the conditional bar determines what information the expectation conditions on.

missing_obligations.closes: Makes the counterfactual comparison well posed.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: Definitions of the baseline path and the action path used in the incremental NPV.
missing_obligations.why_missing: An incremental NPV is a contrast; without both paths, the delta notation is ambiguous.

missing_obligations.closes: Justifies replacing total incremental cash flow by the listed component sum.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: A statement that the listed cash-flow components are exhaustive and share the same sign convention.
missing_obligations.why_missing: A cash-flow decomposition is an accounting identity only after omitted components and sign conventions are ruled out.

missing_obligations.closes: Makes the finite-horizon valuation expression well defined.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: Definitions of the horizon, discount factors, and terminal-value term.
missing_obligations.why_missing: The NPV sum depends on time indexing, discounting, and the terminal payoff convention.

missing_route_assumptions.text: conditional expectation law is defined and the random payoff terms are integrable

next_audit.purpose: Generate explicit assumption proposals for the missing route conditions.

semantic_work_packet.non_claim: Semantic work packets are deterministic guidance for proof search; they are not proof certificates.

assumptions: The conditioned shock or path has finite support.
Every payoff/value term inside the expectation is finite at each support point.
The conditioning state or information set is explicitly defined.
possible_assumption_sets.closes: The expectation becomes a finite weighted sum.

assumptions: A conditional kernel or probability law is fixed for the random object.
All random terms inside the expectation are measurable under that law.
Those terms are dominated by an integrable envelope or have finite conditional first moments.
possible_assumption_sets.closes: The expectation is a well-defined finite conditional integral.

assumptions: The baseline and action paths are defined on the same horizon and information set.
All cash-flow components use the same currency, time index, and sign convention.
Discount factors and terminal value are finite and defined for the horizon.
possible_assumption_sets.closes: The incremental NPV expression is a finite, aligned accounting/valuation identity.

route_required_assumptions.text: conditional expectation law is defined and the random payoff terms are integrable

report_sections.problem:
The target `\Delta \NPV_i(a;d,s,\pi) = -C_i^{\mathrm{acq}}(a)
  +
  \E\!\left[
    \sum_{h=0}^{H}\delta_h\Delta CF_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s)
    +\delta_H\Delta TV_{i,t+H}(a,\pi;s)
    \mid X_{it}^{d}
  \right]` exhausted the branch-search budget before proof or refutation.

semantic_work_packet.label: eq:ss-bellman
semantic_work_packet.location: credit_card_npv_component_proposal_final_submission.tex > Dynamic State-Space Model and Policy Simulation > Observation, Filtering, and Policy Value > Reward mapping and constrained belief-state policy value > How the baseline path enters the dynamic recursion. > eq:ss-bellman > line 4067

missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: Definitions of state, belief state, feasible action set, and nonemptiness of the feasible action set.
missing_obligations.why_missing: A Bellman maximization is not well posed until its domain and feasible controls are defined.

missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: A transition law for next-period states conditional on current state and action.
missing_obligations.why_missing: The continuation value is an expectation over next states; that expectation needs a transition kernel.

missing_obligations.closes: Makes the Bellman objective finite for comparison across actions.
missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: Finite reward and integrable continuation value under each admissible action.
missing_obligations.why_missing: The objective may be undefined or infinite without boundedness or integrability conditions.

missing_obligations.mathematically_missing: A terminal, transversality, or boundary condition for the recursive value.
missing_obligations.why_missing: A recursion cannot be checked as a dynamic-programming equation without a boundary condition.

next_audit.purpose: Regenerate concrete proposals after adding the listed obligations.

semantic_work_packet.non_claim: Semantic work packets are deterministic guidance for proof search; they are not proof certificates.

assumptions: The state and action sets are finite or compact with a nonempty feasible set.
Rewards are finite and measurable.
A transition matrix or kernel is specified for each admissible action.
A terminal value or contraction condition is stated.
possible_assumption_sets.closes: The Bellman operator is well defined and can be audited as a dynamic-programming recursion.

blockers.problem: A transition law for next-period states conditional on current state and action.

backend_attempts.boundary: A branch can be promoted to proved or refuted only from scoped certifying backend evidence or a concrete counterexample. Route plans, retrieval hits, static extraction, proof-state traces, and backend unavailability are diagnostic evidence only.

blockers.problem: Lean certification was selected but no Lean source was supplied.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Supply Lean source or a formalization branch before Lean certification.
blockers.why: Direct Lean checking requires an explicit Lean statement/proof artifact.

blockers.problem: Counterexample search requires a target equality with lhs and rhs.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Provide lhs/rhs or formalize the target as an equality.
blockers.why: The controller could not split the target into non-empty lhs/rhs expressions.

blockers.problem: sympy did not certify or refute the target.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Provide a certifying backend result, concrete counterexample, formalization, or stronger assumption set.
blockers.why: sympy adapter call failed: ValueError: derive_or_refute requires lhs/rhs or a target containing '='

blockers.problem: A conditional probability law for the random variables inside the expectation.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Makes the expectation operator well defined.
blockers.why: A conditional expectation is not a real-valued operator until the conditioning law or kernel is specified.

blockers.problem: Measurability and finite conditional first moments for every payoff, value, or derivative term inside the expectation.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Turns the displayed expression into a finite scalar equality.
blockers.why: Without measurability and integrability, the expectation may be undefined or infinite.

blockers.problem: A definition of the conditioning information set, state, or sigma-field.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Fixes the scope of the conditional expectation used in the derivation.
blockers.why: The notation after the conditional bar determines what information the expectation conditions on.

blockers.problem: Definitions of state, belief state, feasible action set, and nonemptiness of the feasible action set.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Makes the optimization domain explicit.
blockers.why: A Bellman maximization is not well posed until its domain and feasible controls are defined.

blockers.problem: A transition law for next-period states conditional on current state and action.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Defines the stochastic continuation operator.
blockers.why: The continuation value is an expectation over next states; that expectation needs a transition kernel.

blockers.problem: Finite reward and integrable continuation value under each admissible action.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Makes the Bellman objective finite for comparison across actions.
blockers.why: The objective may be undefined or infinite without boundedness or integrability conditions.

blockers.problem: A terminal, transversality, or boundary condition for the recursive value.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Closes the recursive definition.
blockers.why: A recursion cannot be checked as a dynamic-programming equation without a boundary condition.

blockers.problem: The localized source row has extraction uncertainty.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Recover a complete source-local obligation before promoting a derivation.
blockers.why: Equation locator reported: alignment_markers_preserved.

blockers.problem: The label spans multiple localized equation rows.
blockers.required_next_evidence: Group all rows for the label into one formal obligation or split them into separately labeled obligations.
blockers.why: A single-row tree attempt cannot certify a full multiline align environment.

promotion.boundary: A branch can be promoted to proved or refuted only from scoped certifying backend evidence or a concrete counterexample. Route plans, retrieval hits, static extraction, proof-state traces, and backend unavailability are diagnostic evidence only.
promotion.reason: No certifying proof/refutation evidence supports promotion.

controller.reason: Controller stopped with diagnostic evidence, blockers, or exhausted budget.

derivation_steps.justification: Specify the kernel or conditional distribution used by the expectation.
Verify each random payoff, value, or derivative term has a finite conditional expectation.
Only after those checks should the equality be treated as a scalar derivation step.
Specify the domain over which the maximum is taken.
State the reward map and transition law used to form expected continuation value.
Under finiteness/integrability and a boundary condition, evaluate reward plus discounted expected continuation value.
